params.name: Leyden Jar
params.description:
On Mar 7, 1752 AD, the Leyden Jar was created and used as a crude power source.
A Leyden Jar is a device used to store static electricity. As a primitive form of capacitor, it was once an important source of power for electrical experiments. The famous Franklin kite experiment used the Leyden Jar to collect the current on the kite line and then verify that its nature is the same as the artificially obtained current after other experiments. The advent of the Leyden Jar marked the start of elec …
